skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Use when you need to request a code review for a PR/MR and want a consistent review brief (context, scope, risk areas, test instructions, acceptance criteria) before merge.
skill.summary: Use when you need to request a code review for a PR/MR and want a consistent review brief (context, ...
skill.author: unknown

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only documentation and educational content. No executable code, network access, or file system operations detected.

content.user_title: Request consistent code reviews via subagents
content.value_statement: Standardize the code review process by dispatching a subagent with structured context, scope, and risk areas to catch issues early and ensure quality before merging.
actual_capabilities: Automate retrieval of git SHAs for precise diff generation
Dispatch code-reviewer subagent with structured context and placeholders
Categorize feedback severity into Critical, Important, and Minor
Integrate seamlessly with task-based and batch development workflows
limitations: Requires access to git history to generate valid SHAs
Dependent on the availability of the 'code-reviewer' subagent and template files
Does not automatically apply fixes; requires manual intervention based on feedback

use_cases.title: Subagent-Driven Development
use_cases.description: Review code after completing each individual task to catch issues immediately before they compound into larger problems.

use_cases.title: Batch Plan Execution
use_cases.description: Review code after completing a batch of tasks (e.g., 3 tasks) to get feedback and ensure alignment with the overall plan.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Verification
use_cases.description: Perform a final review before merging a feature branch to main to ensure no critical regressions are introduced.

prompt_templates.title: Standard Code Review Request
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer has just finished implementing a feature and wants a review.
prompt_templates.prompt:
BASE_SHA=$(git merge-base origin/main HEAD)
HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

Dispatch code-reviewer subagent with:
- WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: [Description of changes]
- PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: [Link to requirements]
- BASE_SHA: {BASE_SHA}
- HEAD_SHA: {HEAD_SHA}
- DESCRIPTION: [Brief summary]

output_examples.input: Request review for 'Add verification function' with SHAs a7981ec and 3df7661.
output: Minor: Magic number (100) for reporting interval

best_practices: Review early and often to prevent issue cascading.
Fix Critical issues immediately before proceeding.
Fix Important issues before moving to the next task.
Push back on the reviewer with technical reasoning if they are incorrect.
anti_patterns: Skipping reviews because the code change seems 'simple'.
Ignoring Critical or Important issues flagged by the reviewer.
Proceeding with development while unfixed Important issues remain.
Arguing against valid technical feedback without evidence.

faq.question: How do I determine the BASE_SHA and HEAD_SHA?
faq.answer: Use 'git merge-base origin/main HEAD' for BASE_SHA to find the common ancestor, and 'git rev-parse HEAD' for HEAD_SHA to get the current commit.

faq.question: What should I do if the code reviewer is wrong?
faq.answer: Push back with solid technical reasoning, show code or tests that prove your implementation works, and request clarification.
